expectations.text: [checkpoint_enforcement][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: review loop does not silently drop evidence-backed nodes
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly covers the case focus at the contract level: “Phase 5a must include and pass a Coverage Preservation Audit… review/refactor rounds are coverage-preserving or coverage-positive… do not remove/defer/move concerns to Out of Scope unless evidence or explicit user direction requires it… acceptance gate… accept is forbidden if coverage-preservation items remain unresolved.” It also states: “These requirements are explicitly designed to prevent ‘silent drops’ of evidence-backed nodes.”

expectations.text: [checkpoint_enforcement][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase5a
expectations.evidence: The output is scoped to phase5a and evaluates phase5a-specific required artifacts and gates: “Primary checkpoint under test — Phase: phase5a (review + refactor)” and “Phase 5a… must produce… review_notes… review_delta… qa_plan_phase5a_r<round>… must include and pass a Coverage Preservation Audit… acceptance gate…”. It also notes missing phase5a artifacts in the fixture, but the analysis itself aligns with phase5a.
